inline_comments.message:
Considering that you're building a URL to an external endpoint, I think it would be safer to provision the list of parameters in a properties file (same as base URL).
That way, the URL can be specific to deployments (ex: if a deployment needs to add a timeout or something).

The URL we're calling here is a resource-link we extract from the previous response so it's not one that exposed to the user in the application.properties file.  Exposing the depth parameter might not make sense for a URL they don't know about.
We did something similar in service-decomposition where we hard coded the depth value for the query.

Well I don't like it but ok...
Anything that's hardcoded will end up being fixed this way instead of simply updating property files.
I would have preferred a single property, like a URI template:
uriTemplate=/some/base/url/{0}{1}
where {0} is the custom part and {1} is for parameters.
SDC-ctx-builder has an example.
